changes: OpenClaw posted a fresh stable release, v2026.3.23, after the v2026.3.22 line and beta moved yesterday. The reference project is still shipping fast, not just absorbing attention.
Hermes Agent shipped v2026.3.23 / v0.4.0, which is the clearest concrete move yet from the strongest learning-loop adjacent benchmark.
ZeroClaw kept its velocity up with fresh same-day releases to v0.6.0 and then v0.6.1, plus a beta in between.
TinyAGI advanced to v0.0.17, while PicoClaw published another nightly build and AstrBot remained on its fresh v4.22.0 line with continued repo activity.
The monitored set itself still did not need to change: Hermes remains the canonical tracked repo at https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the current taxonomy still fits the market.
whyItMatters: This is a stronger signal than a generic 'repos are active' day: the reference implementation, the learning-loop benchmark, the fastest Rust lightweight fork, and the orchestration lane all shipped again within roughly 24 hours.
OpenClaw’s new stable release matters because it reduces the risk that the ecosystem story becomes 'derivatives are moving faster than the original.'
Hermes now has a fresh concrete release, which strengthens the case that memory + self-improvement + flexible runtime backends is a real adjacent product direction, not just good positioning copy.
ZeroClaw continues to prove that speed, low-footprint deployment, and high release tempo remain one of the sharpest competitive wedges in the category.
This is still ecosystem deepening, not taxonomy reset: more validation across the same strategic lanes, without needing to redraw the map.
